notes: AES internal registers externally readable through AXI-visible wrapper registers.
DMA programming registers are writable without lock mediation.
Register lock state resets to unlocked values and is exported to protected wrappers.
SHA input registers remain readable through wrapper after computation path.
AES plaintext registers remain readable through wrapper after encryption path.
Access-control memory resets to full access and feeds peripheral access gates.
PKT default read handling can expose fuse_rdata_i.
HMAC access gate depends on reset-open access-control state for privilege entries.
PKT access can be enabled by HMAC access-control bit.
ACCT write map references invalid or ineffective lock bits for some entries.
JTAG unlock participates in reglk reset condition and clears locks.
we_flag can force access-control output bits independent of stored policy.

permission_security_objective: AES key exposure through default read
notes: AES0 read path can retain key0 data through incomplete default handling.
AES2 access gate depends on reset-open privilege-indexed access-control state.
PKT fuse request and address registers are writable without lock guards.
SHA protected data registers use inconsistent lock bits for read and write mediation.
CLINT bridge lacks explicit access-control gating.
AES0 masks key0 and key1 in debug mode but leaves key2 unmasked.
HMAC key-derived registers use inconsistent lock bits for write and read protection.
AES1 key registers use inconsistent lock bits for write and read protection.
HMAC reset block does not clear key and key-derived registers.
AES1 leaves one key path unmasked under debug mode.
